headlines.title: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
headlines.description: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

headlines.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
headlines.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

headlines.title: Watch: Melania Trump's surprise Epstein statement in full
headlines.description: She tells reporters at the White House that any claims linking the two "need to end today".

headlines.title: How the cost of holidays, food and clothes is rising - despite the Iran ceasefire
headlines.description: Fuel costs have already soared, flights and mortgages are also rising - and food could soon do the same.

headlines.title: EU fingerprint and photo travel rules come into force
headlines.description: The EU's much-delayed Entry/Exit System will change the way UK passengers travel to 29 countries.

headlines.title: Want to help garden birds? Don't feed them in warmer months, says RSPB
headlines.description: The UK's largest bird charity has issued new guidance advising people to stop using feeders to help wildlife thrive.

headlines.title: Co-leader McIlroy's golf does the talking after busy Masters build-up
headlines.description: With the pomp and celebration of his Masters victory finished, Rory McIlroy reminded everyone he is back at Augusta to win again.

headlines.title: Iran conflict must be 'line in sand' to build more resilient UK, Starmer says
headlines.description: The prime minister says "shocks" like the conflict in Iran are becoming more frequent.

headlines.title: Ten cases a day - 'blitz courts' could tackle the Crown Court backlog
headlines.description: The scheme, to fast-track cases and to cut the court backlog, is being expanded in England and Wales.

headlines.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to truce for Orthodox Easter
headlines.description: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon on 11 April through Easter Sunday.

headlines.title: Hip-hop pioneer Afrika Bambaataa dies aged 68
headlines.description: The Bronx-born rapper's global hit Planet Rock is credited with shaping hip-hop in the 1980s.

headlines.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
headlines.description: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

headlines.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
headlines.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

headlines.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
headlines.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

headlines.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
headlines.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

headlines.title: Can stats help you find the Grand National winner?
headlines.description: How might data help you pick the winner from the 34 horses racing in Saturday's Grand National? BBC Sport delves into the facts and figures in search of an answer.

headlines.title: This coat cost $248 in illegal tariffs. Will he ever get the money back?
headlines.description: Importers are in line for tariff refunds. But whether everyone who paid the for the tariffs will get money back is a trickier question.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: What might have made Paddington panic about his marmalade?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: How the Artemis crew will splash down on Earth
headlines.description: The Artemis crew will return to Earth on 10 April after a 10-day mission that took them around the Moon.

headlines.title: The Papers:  'I'm not Epstein's victim' and 'We see you, Vlad'
headlines.description: US First Lady Melania Trump is splashed across several of the papers, after she made a surprise White House address on Thursday.

headlines.title: Travellers told to allow more time for journeys as Irish fuel protests continue
headlines.description: The Irish military will be deployed to move vehicles "blocking critical infrastructure".

headlines.title: 'I was in a slump - now my art is in Billie Eilish's house'
headlines.description: Sylvie Baker says she can look for studio space after the US star's purchase gave her more exposure.

headlines.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
headlines.description: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

headlines.title: Singapore government employees asked to work in 25C to save on air-con costs
headlines.description: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

headlines.title: 'The road to our home must be the worst in the UK'
headlines.description: The Byatt family say parts of the Upper Coquet valley road are falling into the river.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Defence secretary interview on Russian submarine operation
headlines.description: British warship and aircraft deployed to deter Russian submarine action.

headlines.title: Trump pulls back from the brink...but for how long?
headlines.description: Trump’s threat to “wipe out” Iran has divided Republicans.

headlines.title: Co-leader McIlroy's golf does the talking after busy Masters build-up
headlines.description: With the pomp and celebration of his Masters victory finished, Rory McIlroy reminded everyone he is back at Augusta to win again.

headlines.title: Stojsavljevic, 17, wins as GB take charge in BJK Cup
headlines.description: Teenager Mika Stojsavljevic stuns Talia Gibson, ranked more than 200 places above her, in straight sets as Great Britain take a 2-0 lead over Australia in the Billie Jean King Cup.

headlines.title: Six big questions for Fury's fifth return from retirement
headlines.description: Key voices from boxing weigh in on the big questions surrounding heavyweight Tyson Fury's latest comeback.

headlines.title: MacIntyre faces action after angry Masters gesture
headlines.description: Scotland's Robert MacIntyre could face disciplinary action at The Masters after an angry gesture at the 15th green during a quadruple bogey.

headlines.title: 'Hungry' Watkins is man on mission - but will Tuchel take notice?
headlines.description: Ollie Watkins' response to being dropped by England has been nothing short of emphatic, with two strikes in Bologna adding to his goal against West Ham before the international break.
